text:
Once upon a time, in a sleepy town named Dreamhill, there lived a clever mouse named Merlin. Merlin loved learning about things. He especially loved dreams. Every night before bedtime, he would climb up his grandma's feathered pillow and listen to her whispers about adventures in faraway places.

One moonlit night, Merlin dreamed he could fly. In his dream, he hopped onto wings made of moth powder. With fluffy feathers guiding his wings, he soared above Dreamhill. He could see everything—from ivies climbing walls to crows having tea parties.

But Merlin was greedy. He wanted more. He dreamed of flying to stars. So he stretched his arms wide. Lo and behold! He floated! But being silly silly Merlin didn't know how. He floated down.

Down he came smack! onto Mrs. Todder's umbrella. "Whoaa!" she shouted.

Merlin felt stupid. Why didn't he think?

Later, he dreamed he learned to read stars. Nightly
